cards.q: Chapter 12 का शीर्षक क्या है?
cards.a: Moving Data: Data Pump, SQL*Loader (الصفحة 2)

cards.q: डेटा मूव करने के लिए उपयोग किए जाने वाले दो मुख्य उपकरण कौन से हैं?
cards.a: Data Pump और SQL*Loader (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: Data Pump का उपयोग करने से पहले क्या बनाना आवश्यक है?
cards.a: Directory Objects (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: डेटा पंप के मुख्य कार्य कौन से हैं?
cards.a: Taking an Export/Import, Performing a Data Pump Export, और Performing a Data Pump Import (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: What is one of the objectives regarding data movement covered in the textbook?
cards.a: Data Pump Architecture (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: Besides general data movement, what advanced concept involving exporting and importing is a key objective?
cards.a: Exporting and Importing with Granularity (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: Which tool's usage for loading data from non-Oracle databases is listed as an objective?
cards.a: Use SQL*Loader to load data (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: What technique is covered for unloading and loading data?
cards.a: Unloading and Loading Data Using an External Table (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: What API contains utilities for high-speed export and import of bulk data and metadata movement?
cards.a: DBMS_DATAPUMP (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: Which Oracle Database interface minimizes data conversion and parsing at both unload and load time?
cards.a: Direct Path API (DPAPI) (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: What utility is used for unloading data and metadata into a set of operating system files called dump file sets?
cards.a: Data Pump Export (الصفحة 9)

cards.q: What table, created in the schema of the user running the Data Pump job, contains all aspects of the job?
cards.a: Master Table (MT) (الصفحة 9)

cards.q: What is Oracle Data Pump?
cards.a: A server-based facility for high-speed data and metadata movement (الصفحة 10)

cards.q: What are the command-line export and import clients used with Data Pump?
cards.a: expdp and impdp (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: When does Data Pump use external tables rather than direct path for data movement?
cards.a: If there are clustered tables, referential integrity constraints, or encrypted columns (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: Name one benefit of using Oracle Data Pump.
cards.a: Fine-grained object and data selection, parallel execution, or network mode in a distributed environment (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: ما هي كائنات الدليل (Directory objects) في Data Pump؟
cards.a: هياكل منطقية تمثل دليلًا ماديًا على نظام ملفات الخادم، وتسمح باستخدام اسم الكائن بدلاً من تحديد مسار الدليل بشكل ثابت (الصفحة 15).

cards.q: ما هو الغرض من استخدام المعامل COMPRESSION في Data Pump؟
cards.a: لتحديد ما إذا كان يجب ضغط البيانات الوصفية (metadata) في ملف التصدير، مما يقلل من مساحة القرص واستهلاكها (الصفحة 14).

cards.q: ما هو المعامل المستخدم لتقدير المساحة التي سيستهلكها تصدير البيانات دون إجراء التصدير الفعلي؟
cards.a: ESTIMATE_ONLY (الصفحة 14).

cards.q: ما هو المعامل الذي يحدد الحد الأقصى لعدد خيوط خوادم التنفيذ النشطة التي تعمل نيابة عن مهمة التصدير؟
cards.a: PARALLEL (الصفحة 14).

cards.q: What utility is used for unloading data and metadata into a set of operating system files called dump file sets?
cards.a: Data Pump Export (الصفحة 18)

cards.q: Which utility is used to load metadata and data stored in an export dump file set into a target system?
cards.a: Data Pump Import (الصفحة 18)

cards.q: Why are Directory Objects required when specifying file locations for Data Pump?
cards.a: Because it accesses files on the server rather than on the client. (الصفحة 16)

cards.q: What is the Master Table (MT) in a Data Pump operation, and what does it maintain?
cards.a: A table created in the schema of the user running the Data Pump job that maintains all aspects of the job. (الصفحة 18)

cards.q: What are the interfaces used to interact with Data Pump Export and Import?
cards.a: Command-line interface, Parameter file interface, Interactive–command interface, and Enterprise Manager Cloud Control/Toad for Oracle. (الصفحة 20)

cards.q: List the different modes of Data Pump Export and Import.
cards.a: Full, Schema, Table, Tablespace, Transportable tablespace, and Transportable database. (الصفحة 20)

cards.q: What does the Command-line interface enable for Data Pump?
cards.a: Specifying most of the export parameters directly on the command line. (الصفحة 21)

cards.q: According to the text, what is the function of the Parameter file interface in Data Pump?
cards.a: It enables specifying all command–line parameters in a parameter file; the only exception is the PARFILE parameter. (الصفحة 21)

cards.q: What tools can be used to make a Data Pump export?
cards.a: Using Enterprise Manager Cloud Control or Toad for Oracle (الصفحة 22)

cards.q: From which menu should a user begin to perform a Data Pump export session using the GUI?
cards.a: Expand the Schema menu, select Database Export/Import (الصفحة 24)

cards.q: If a privileged user is connected to the database instance, what are the available export types in Data Pump?
cards.a: Database, Schemas, Tables, and Tablespace (الصفحة 24)

cards.q: Which utility is illustrated for performing a Data Pump import using the command line?
cards.a: The `impdp` utility (الصفحة 24)

cards.q: What makes it easy to apply transformations when DDL is being formed during Data Pump Import?
cards.a: Object metadata is stored as XML in the dump file set. (الصفحة 26)

cards.q: When is `REMAP_DATAFILE` useful?
cards.a: It is useful when moving databases across platforms that have different file-system semantics. (الصفحة 26)

cards.q: What capability does `REMAP_SCHEMA` provide?
cards.a: It provides the old FROMUSER /TOUSER capability to change object ownership. (الصفحة 26)

cards.q: Which transformation provides the ability to rename entire tables?
cards.a: REMAP_TABLE. (الصفحة 26)

cards.q: What is the primary function of SQL\*Loader?
cards.a: Loads data from external files into tables of an Oracle database using a powerful data parsing engine. (الصفحة 28)

cards.q: Name three possible record formats for a data file read by SQL\*Loader.
cards.a: Fixed record format, variable record format, or stream record format. (الصفحة 28)

cards.q: What are the three sections that constitute a control file?
cards.a: Session-wide information (Global options), INTO TABLE blocks (containing table and column info), and optionally, input data log file. (الصفحة 30)

cards.q: How does SQL\*Loader determine if a record goes into the "Bad file" versus being filtered to the "Discard file"?
cards.a: The bad file contains records rejected due to invalid input format or determined invalid by the Oracle database; the discard file contains records filtered out because they do not match any record-selection criteria. (الصفحة 30)

cards.q: What aspects does the SQL*Loader control file instruct about?
cards.a: Location of the data to be loaded, Data format, Configuration details, Memory management, Record rejection, Interrupted load handling details, and Data manipulation details (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: What type of language does the SQL*Loader control file contain instructions in?
cards.a: Data definition language (DDL) (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: Name three aspects controlled by the SQL*Loader control file regarding configuration.
cards.a: Memory management, selection and rejection criteria, and interrupted load handling (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: What is the purpose of the SQL*Loader control file?
cards.a: It is a text file that contains DDL instructions used to control various aspects of a SQL*Loader session (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: Which path load uses COMMIT?
cards.a: Conventional Load (الصفحة 36)

cards.q: Which path load provides data saves, resulting in a faster operation?
cards.a: Direct Path Load (الصفحة 36)

cards.q: Which path load fires INSERT triggers?
cards.a: Conventional Load (الصفحة 36)

cards.q: How does Direct Path Load merge new index entries?
cards.a: Merges new index entries at the end of the load (الصفحة 36)

cards.q: What method executes SQL INSERT statements to populate tables in an Oracle database?
cards.a: A conventional path load (الصفحة 38)

cards.q: How does a direct path load eliminate overhead when writing data blocks?
cards.a: By formatting Oracle data blocks and writing the data blocks directly to the database files (الصفحة 38)

cards.q: What is one key difference between a data save and a COMMIT operation regarding transactions?
cards.a: A data save does not end the transaction, while conventional path loads use a database COMMIT operation for saving data (الصفحة 38)

cards.q: After a data save, what happens to the High-Water Mark (HWM)?
cards.a: The HWM is moved (الصفحة 38)
